title: 10 medicinal constituents of Angelica dahurica (baikal angelica)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, berberine is a bioactive compound extracted from the root of the plant. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. Berberine exhibits antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it effective against bacterial, fungal, and viral infections, as well as cardiovascular diseases. Its potential benefits also extend to diabetes management and weight loss.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, Sabine is a perennial plant native to eastern Asia. It belongs to the carrot family and is widely used in traditional Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant's roots and leaves are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, oxypeucedanin is a bioactive compound isolated from the root of Angelica dahurica, a plant native to Asia. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Oxypeucedanin has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for the treatment of various diseases. Its unique chemical structure also makes it a valuable tool in research on plant bioactive compounds.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, Imperatorin is a bioactive compound extracted from the plant Angelica dahurica. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Imperatorin has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, making it a promising agent in modern pharmaceutical applications.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, phellandrene is a bioactive compound extracted from the roots of Angelica dahurica, a plant native to China and Russia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Phellandrene has also been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it a promising natural remedy for modern health concerns.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, asarone is a bioactive compound extracted from the root of the plant Angelica dahurica. Asarone has been traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Recent studies have shown that asarone possesses significant anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it a potential natural remedy for several modern diseases.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, lactone is a bioactive compound extracted from the root of A. dahurica, a plant native to China and Russia. It has been traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Angelica dahurica lactone has been shown to exhibit various pharmacological activities, including anti-allergic, anti-bacterial, and anti-viral effects, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of various diseases.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, Kaempferol is a bioactive flavonoid compound extracted from the root of this plant. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Recent studies have revealed its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, quercetin is a flavonoid compound derived from the root of the plant. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Quercetin has been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable ingredient in natural health supplements.

Angelica dahurica, also known as Baikal angelica, chlorogenic acid is a type of bioactive compound extracted from the roots and rhizomes of this plant. This acid has been extensively studied for its various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Chlorogenic acid has been found to exhibit potential therapeutic effects in treating various diseases, such as cardiovascular disorders, cancer, and diabetes, making it a promising area of research in the field of traditional Chinese medicine.

intro: Angelica dahurica has active constituents such as lignans, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The constituents of Angelica dahurica can help alleviate various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. For instance, its anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling and pain in joints, while its antiseptic properties can speed up wound healing, thereby improving quality of life for individuals suffering from these conditions.
intro_desc: Angelica dahurica has active constituents such as volatile oils, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes. These constituents contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The volatile oils can help alleviate digestive issues and reduce stress, while the alkaloids may aid in lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. By harnessing these properties, baikal angelica can help people manage various health concerns and improve their overall well-being, leading to a better quality of life.
